The Diriyah Gate Development Authority (DGDA) was established in July 2017 with a mandate to preserve and celebrate historic Diriyah; the Jewel of the Kingdom. It is the home of first Saudi capital, recognized as the symbolic birthplace of the nation, and an inspiration for the generations of leaders that have led the Kingdom. Diriyah, and its UNESCO World Heritage Site At-Turaif, is known for magnificent mud-brick structures in the Najdi architectural style. It is a place where history lives and breathes and visitors can walk in the footsteps of kings and heroes through incomparable scenery. DGDA, led by CEO Jerry Inzerillo, seeks to build the future of Diriyah on the foundation of its bright past, and to provide visitors to this great gathering place with an extraordinary and historical getaway in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia. We work to develop Diriyahâ€™s architectural, artistic and economic dimensions and to reinforce and enhance the social and cultural values that have shaped modern Saudi Arabia. In order to make Diriyah one of the worldâ€™s great gathering places, we at DGDA are committed to create a world-class cultural heritage destination for our people and for visitors looking to discover the essence of Saudi Arabia. In line with Vision 2030, we strive to foster a vibrant community that will help to create job opportunities for young Saudis who are passionate about preserving the great history of our country. In line with its ambitious growth plans, DGDA incorporated the Diriyah Development Company (DevCo) in April 2021. The subsidiary entity will drive progress for the projectâ€™s core purpose forward. Honoring its rich history and architecture, DGDA will work to make Diriyah a vibrant economic destination that combines historical characteristics and cultural legacy with luxurious local and international brands.

The Company for Cooperative Insurance (Tawuniya) is a Saudi Joint Stock Company established in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia by Royal Decree number M/5 of 17.4.1405H (5.4.1985 G) and was registered on January 18, 1986G (7.5.1406H) under Commercial Registration number 1010061695. Its principal lines of business include motor, marine, fire, medical, engineering, energy, aviation, takaful, and casualty insurance. The authorized, issued and paid up capital of the Company is SR 1000 million consisting of 100 million shares of SR 10 each. The capital ownership is as follows: The General Organization for Social Insurance 23%, the Public Pension Agency 24%, and 53% held by the public through stock market. Tawuniya has the resources and means to extend its products to meet retail and corporate needs while simultaneously transacting in various classes of risks, whether simple or complicated. Upon launching any new insurance covers, Tawuniya strives to be attentive to its clients and to react to their demands. It always attempts to gauge the public's response to Tawuniya's media messages in regards to launching or modifying various insurance covers or implementing new products.
